prompt: Commercial food advertising photography, hyper-detailed and appetizing. Seamless saturated studio background in flat chili red #C8301B, with a subtle darker vignette at the bottom edge and slightly lighter glow upper-left. Punchy directional studio key light from upper left, crisp defined edges, crisp soft-edged drop shadow. Small crumbs and particles suspended around the subject. Colors rich and saturated but food tones natural and true. The subject: a premium matte stand-up snack pouch in the exact same chili red as the background, floating upright in mid-air tilted about 6 degrees, separated from the background only by its crisp drop shadow and a bright edge highlight. Bold minimalist color-block packaging design printed in cream #F6EDDD ink: at the top an elegant high-contrast serif logotype reading exactly "Seema", directly beneath it a small Devanagari word reading exactly "सीमा", below that the product name stacked huge across two lines in the same cream serif reading exactly "Poha chivda", beneath it one small monospace line reading exactly "no palm oil · no preservatives", and in the bottom corner "250 g" in small monospace. In the lower third of the pouch a neat circular clear window reveals the real product inside: authentic Maharashtrian poha chivda made from FLAT paper-thin pressed-rice poha flakes — matte, completely flat, irregular ragged oval outlines, so thin their edges are slightly translucent, pale turmeric-gold, lying in loose flat layers — mixed with roasted peanuts, dark green fried curry leaves, one raisin, thin sev noodle strands and tiny black mustard seeds. Absolutely NOT cornflakes: no corrugated flakes, no glossy curled breakfast-cereal flakes, no thick chips. Beside the pouch, three flat paper-thin poha flakes, one roasted peanut and one fried curry leaf float in the air. All label text perfectly legible, correctly spelled, cleanly typeset, straight.
